subject: general-knowledge
description: International Boundaries MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: The Durand Line is the boundary between which two countries?
mcqs.explanation: The Durand Line is the boundary between Pakistan and Afghanistan, demarcated in 1893.

mcqs.q: In which year was the Durand Line demarcated?
mcqs.explanation: The Durand Line was demarcated on 12 November 1893 by Sir Mortimer Durand and Amir Abdur Rahman Khan.

mcqs.q: What is the total length of the Durand Line (Pakistan-Afghanistan frontier)?
mcqs.explanation: The total length of the Durand Line / Pakistan-Afghanistan frontier is approximately 2,252 km.

mcqs.q: Who demarcated the Durand Line between British India and Afghanistan?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Mortimer Durand (then Foreign Secretary of British India) demarcated the line in 1893 — hence its name.

mcqs.q: With which Afghan ruler was the Durand Line agreement signed in 1893?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Mortimer Durand signed the agreement with Amir Abdur Rahman Khan of Afghanistan in 1893.

mcqs.q: The Radcliffe Line is the boundary between which two countries?
mcqs.explanation: The Radcliffe Line demarcates the boundary between India and Pakistan at the time of Partition in 1947.

mcqs.q: In which year was the Radcliffe Line drawn?
mcqs.explanation: The Radcliffe Line was drawn at the time of Partition in August 1947.

mcqs.q: Who drew the Radcliffe Line dividing India and Pakistan?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Cyril Radcliffe, a British lawyer, chaired the Boundary Commission and drew the line in just over a month.

mcqs.q: Which two provinces of British India did the Radcliffe Line divide?
mcqs.explanation: The Radcliffe Line divided the provinces of Punjab and Bengal between India and Pakistan.

mcqs.q: The McMahon Line is the boundary between which two countries?
mcqs.explanation: The McMahon Line is the boundary between India and China, drawn at the 1914 Simla Convention.

mcqs.q: At which historic convention was the McMahon Line drawn?
mcqs.explanation: The McMahon Line was drawn at the 1914 Simla Convention by Sir Henry McMahon, then British India's Foreign Secretary.

mcqs.q: Who drew the McMahon Line?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Henry McMahon, then Foreign Secretary of British India, drew the line at the 1914 Simla Convention.

mcqs.q: The Line of Control (LoC) is the working boundary between India and Pakistan in which region?
mcqs.explanation: The Line of Control (LoC) is the working boundary between India and Pakistan in the disputed Kashmir region.

mcqs.q: The 24th Parallel is a boundary line situated between which two countries?
mcqs.explanation: The 24th Parallel is a boundary line between Pakistan and India, settled after the Rann of Kutch dispute (1968).

mcqs.q: In which dispute was the 24th Parallel established as a boundary between Pakistan and India?
mcqs.explanation: In the 1965 Rann of Kutch dispute, Pakistan declared the 24th Parallel as the frontier with India — settled in 1968.

mcqs.q: The 38th Parallel is the boundary line between which two countries?
mcqs.explanation: The 38th Parallel is the boundary between North Korea and South Korea, set after WWII in 1945.

mcqs.q: In which year was the 38th Parallel set as the boundary between the two Koreas?
mcqs.explanation: The 38th Parallel was set after WWII in 1945 — initially as a temporary Soviet-American division of Korea.

mcqs.q: Which conflict in the early 1950s was fought across the 38th Parallel?
mcqs.explanation: The Korean War (1950-1953) was fought across the 38th Parallel between North Korea (backed by China/USSR) and South Korea (backed by UN/US).

mcqs.q: The 49th Parallel is the boundary line between which two countries?
mcqs.explanation: The 49th Parallel (49° N) is the boundary between the USA and Canada — among the world's longest undefended international borders.

mcqs.q: Which boundary line is sometimes called the longest undefended international border?
mcqs.explanation: The 49th Parallel (USA-Canada) is among the longest undefended international borders in the world.

mcqs.q: The 24th and 49th Parallels are often confused in MCQs. Which is between the USA and Canada?
mcqs.explanation: The 49th Parallel is USA-Canada; the 24th Parallel is Pakistan-India. Don't confuse them.

mcqs.q: The 17th Parallel was the former boundary between which two countries?
mcqs.explanation: The 17th Parallel was the former boundary between North and South Vietnam (1954-1975), set at the Geneva Accords.

mcqs.q: The 17th Parallel as a Vietnam boundary was set at which 1954 agreement?
mcqs.explanation: The 1954 Geneva Accords ended the First Indochina War and divided Vietnam at the 17th Parallel.

mcqs.q: The Maginot Line was a fortification built by which country?
mcqs.explanation: The Maginot Line was a French line of fortification built in 1934 along the German border, from Switzerland to Belgium.

mcqs.q: In which year was the Maginot Line built?
mcqs.explanation: The Maginot Line was constructed in 1934, before WWII, to deter a German invasion of France.

mcqs.q: The Maginot Line stretched along the German border between which two countries?
mcqs.explanation: The Maginot Line stretched from Switzerland to Belgium along the French-German border. It was named after French Minister of War André Maginot.

mcqs.q: The Hindenburg Line was a German line of fortification separating Germany from which country?
mcqs.explanation: The Hindenburg Line was a German line of fortification separating Germany from Poland (demarcated 1977).

mcqs.q: The Gustav Line was a World War II German defensive line in which country?
mcqs.explanation: The Gustav Line was a major German defensive line in WWII stretching across central Italy.

mcqs.q: Which line was set up in 1945 as the Polish-Russian frontier?
mcqs.explanation: The Curzon Line — first proposed in 1919 — became the formal Polish-Russian frontier in 1945.

mcqs.q: The Curzon Line was first proposed in which year?
mcqs.explanation: The Curzon Line was originally proposed in 1919 by British Foreign Secretary Lord Curzon. It became the official Polish-Soviet frontier in 1945.

mcqs.q: The Mannheim Line is a German line of fortification separating Germany from which country?
mcqs.explanation: The Mannheim Line was a German fortification line separating Germany from Poland.

mcqs.q: The Hot Line was initially established between which two countries?
mcqs.explanation: The Hot Line was a direct teletype communication line initially established between the USA (Pentagon) and the USSR (Kremlin).

mcqs.q: In which year was the Hot Line first established between the USA and the USSR?
mcqs.explanation: The Hot Line was established in August 1963 following the 1962 Cuban Missile Crisis, to avoid accidental nuclear war.

mcqs.q: The Hot Line was set up to avoid:
mcqs.explanation: The Hot Line was set up to enable instant communication during emergencies and avoid accidental nuclear war.

mcqs.q: Which two countries cut off the Hot Line in March 2013?
mcqs.explanation: North Korea cut the Hot Line with South Korea in March 2013 amid rising military tensions.

mcqs.q: The International Date Line passes through which ocean?
mcqs.explanation: The International Date Line runs through the Pacific Ocean at roughly 180° longitude.

mcqs.q: At approximately which longitude does the International Date Line run?
mcqs.explanation: The International Date Line runs at approximately 180° longitude (with deviations to avoid splitting countries).

mcqs.q: The International Date Line separates:
mcqs.explanation: Crossing the International Date Line involves adding or subtracting a calendar day — it separates two consecutive calendar dates.

mcqs.q: The Plimsoll Line is a safety mark used on:
mcqs.explanation: The Plimsoll Line is a safety load line marked on ships, indicating the maximum safe loading depth.

mcqs.q: The Green Line is associated with ceasefire/buffer zones in conflict areas such as:
mcqs.explanation: Green Lines have been used as ceasefire/buffer-zone boundaries in Cyprus, Lebanon and other conflict areas.

mcqs.q: The English Channel is the natural boundary between which two countries?
mcqs.explanation: The English Channel (La Manche) is the natural sea boundary dividing Britain and France.

mcqs.q: Which of the following are recognised boundary lines between India and Pakistan?
mcqs.explanation: India and Pakistan share the Line of Control (in Kashmir), the Working Boundary and the 24th Parallel (Rann of Kutch).

mcqs.q: Which line is also known as the 'Control Line' between India and Pakistan?
mcqs.explanation: The Line of Control (LoC) is also called the 'Control Line' — the working boundary in Kashmir.

mcqs.q: Which boundary was drawn first?
mcqs.explanation: The Durand Line (1893) was drawn first — before the McMahon Line (1914), Radcliffe Line (1947) and 38th Parallel (1945).

mcqs.q: Which of the following lines separates Britain and France?
mcqs.explanation: The English Channel separates Britain and France — a natural sea boundary, not a man-made line.

mcqs.q: Which line was named after the British Minister for War (1934)?
mcqs.explanation: The Maginot Line is named after André Maginot, French Minister of War, who championed its construction in the 1930s.

mcqs.q: The Pakistan-Afghanistan boundary (Durand Line) is approximately how many kilometres long?
mcqs.explanation: The Pakistan-Afghanistan boundary (Durand Line) is about 2,252 km long.

mcqs.q: Which boundary is associated with the 1914 Simla Convention?
mcqs.explanation: The McMahon Line was drawn at the 1914 Simla Convention. India accepts it; China has historically disputed it.

mcqs.q: Which boundary is associated with the 1962 Cuban Missile Crisis?
mcqs.explanation: The Hot Line was set up after the 1962 Cuban Missile Crisis to avoid accidental nuclear war between the USA and USSR.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT a parallel line used as a boundary?
mcqs.explanation: Durand Line is NOT a parallel — it is a named demarcated land boundary. The 38th, 24th and 49th are all parallels of latitude.
